principle: Before scraping data or source text, first discover existing skills, official APIs, public extraction guides, and source-specific caveats; record why each chosen source is used.

local_skills.name: "openai-docs"
local_skills.description: "Use when the user asks how to build with OpenAI products or APIs and needs up-to-date official documentation with citations, help choosing the latest model for a use case, or model upgrade and prompt-upgrade guidance; prioritize OpenAI docs MCP tools, use bundled references only as helper context, and restrict any fallback browsing to official OpenAI domains."

local_skills.name: ai-org-analysis-add
local_skills.description: Use when working in the AI Org research project and an existing company report needs new essence or source-note evidence added without rebuilding the report.

local_skills.name: ai-org-analysis
local_skills.description: Use when working in the AI Org research project and turning company essence files into detailed analysis markdown, fact packs, complex reports, one-page reports, or organization/investment analysis.

local_skills.name: ai-org-collect
local_skills.description: Use when working in the AI Org research project and starting or repairing company data collection, especially podcasts, financials, Substack, YouTube, transcripts, or vault sync.

local_skills.name: ai-org-essence
local_skills.description: Use when working in the AI Org research project and converting transcripts, podcast notes, YouTube transcripts, Substack articles, or reports into source-level essence files.

local_skills.name: ai-org-financial-modeling
local_skills.description: Launcher for the AI Org public-company financial modeling workflow. Use for DCF/comps workbook generation from collected statements, market data, valuation, peers, and capital signals.

local_skills.name: ai-org-public-company-collect
local_skills.description: Launcher for the AI Org public-company collection workflow. Use for listed-company filings, earnings calls, market data, books, investor views, and semi-public research folders.

local_skills.name: xlsx
local_skills.description: Use when working with Excel-compatible spreadsheet files, including .xlsx, .xls, .csv, and .tsv files; creating, editing, analyzing, formatting, charting, validating formulas, or exporting workbooks.

local_skills.name: analysis-add
local_skills.description: Use when working in the AI Org research project and an existing company report must absorb new source-note evidence without rebuilding the report or changing its established framework.

local_skills.name: analysis
local_skills.description: Use when turning company essence files into detailed analysis markdown under companies/{slug}/analysis, especially long complex reports, fact packs, evidence maps, and organization/investment analysis.

local_skills.name: collect
local_skills.description: Use when starting or repairing a company research collection pipeline for AI org / investment work, especially when the task needs podcasts, financials, Substack, YouTube, transcripts, or vault sync.

local_skills.name: essence
local_skills.description: Use when converting company transcripts, podcast notes, YouTube transcripts, Substack articles, reports, jobs pages, or other source material into source-level notes for AI org or investment research.

local_skills.name: financial-modeling
local_skills.description: Build a source-mapped public-company DCF and comps workbook from collected statements, market data, valuation, peers, and capital signals.

local_skills.name: public-company-collect
local_skills.description: Collect public-company filings, statements, market data, capital-market signals, earnings calls, books, and semi-public research scaffolding for A-share, US, and Hong Kong listed companies.

external_skill_directories.name: SkillsMP marketplace
external_skill_directories.use: Large third-party SKILL.md marketplace with REST search and optional MCP server; inspect source and license before installing.

external_skill_directories.name: OpenAI skills catalog
external_skill_directories.use: First external place to check for Codex-compatible skills and examples.

external_skill_directories.name: The Skills Directory
external_skill_directories.use: Searchable third-party directory for Codex SKILL.md examples.

external_skill_directories.name: skills.sh
external_skill_directories.use: Third-party agent-skill index; use only after license/source review.

market_guides.name: SEC EDGAR APIs
market_guides.use: Canonical submissions and XBRL companyfacts source for US filings and facts.

market_guides.name: SEC daily companyfacts bulk archive
market_guides.use: Bulk fallback when many issuers are collected.

sources.name: Yahoo chart JSON
sources.use: Unauthenticated price history and last-price fallback.

sources.name: StockAnalysis statistics pages
sources.use: Public fallback for market cap, share count, and peer multiples; treat as medium confidence.

sources.name: local collect skill
sources.use: Reuse query-map channels for podcasts, YouTube, Substack, official, jobs, social, and longform.

sources.name: Nasdaq institutional holdings API
sources.use: Free public holder table; retry and curl/http1 fallback because TLS/API availability can be unstable.

sources.name: Holdings Channel by-stock 13F aggregate
sources.use: Free 13F aggregate fallback when Nasdaq API fails.

sources.name: Paid holder APIs
sources.use: Ask the user for a paid API only after official/free/fallback sources fail or coverage is insufficient.

required_next_step: Use official or well-documented sources first; if a source lacks documentation or reproducible extraction rules, write an extraction gap instead of inventing data.
